text: "The fire of the Chaldeans"... The city of Ur in Chaldea, the name of which signifies fire. Or out of the fire of the tribulations and temptations, to which he was there exposed.—The ancient Rabbins understood this literally, affirming that Abram was cast into the fire by the idolaters, and brought out by a miracle without any hurt.
"And gave the head."... That is, they set their head, or were bent to return to Egypt.
